events.event_title: Victim and Mother First Visit Epstein's Palm Beach House for Tea
events.description: Minor victim and her mother visited Epstein's Palm Beach house and had tea with Epstein by his pool after being invited by Epstein's office; Epstein told them he mentored young talented individuals.; Minor victim regularly spent time at Epstein's Palm Beach house from ages 14 to 17; Epstein gave her $200-300 in cash per visit and Maxwell and Epstein groomed her for sexual abuse.; Juan Alessi, Epstein's house manager, was directed by Epstein and Maxwell to pick up the minor victim from her home and drive her to Epstein's Palm Beach house; he also drove Epstein, Maxwell, and the victim to the movies.
events.nexus_reasoning: Epstein was physically present at his Palm Beach house during the visit and personally spoke with the victim and her mother.

evidence.excerpt: Several weeks later, when was home from camp, she recalls someone from Epstein's office calling her house and inviting her to come to Epstein's house in Palm Beach for tea. and her mother went to the house, where they had tea with Epstein by his pool.

subsequently began regularly spending time at Epstein's house in Palm Beach, beginning at age 14, and continuing until she was 17, when she relocated to New York City. During those visits, Epstein regularly gave her $200-300 in cash

Alessi remembers being sent by both Epstein and Maxwell to pick up from her house and drive her to Epstein's house, and he recalls the specific neighborhood where she lived. He recalls Maxwell and Epstein spending time with at the Palm Beach house, and he also remembers being sent to buy movie tickets, and driving Maxwell, and Epstein to the movies.

In approximately 1993, Maxwell began coming to the house in Palm Beach, and became in charge of the whole house. Epstein told Alessi to go to Maxwell for his orders, and he began taking directions from Maxwell about house operations.
